title: VirtualService vs DestinationRule
description:
## VirtualService vs DestinationRule

Two of the most important Istio traffic management resources are `VirtualService` and `DestinationRule`.

Which statement **best** describes their respective roles?

options.text: VirtualService defines subsets of a service; DestinationRule defines how to route to those subsets
VirtualService defines routing rules (how traffic flows); DestinationRule defines policies applied to traffic after routing (subsets, load balancing, TLS)
VirtualService controls mTLS between pods; DestinationRule controls circuit breaking
Both resources are interchangeable; you only need one at a time

explanation: A VirtualService attaches routing rules to a host — it decides *where* traffic goes (weighted routing, retries, fault injection, header matching). A DestinationRule defines policies applied *after* routing: subsets (used to target specific pod versions), load balancing algorithm, connection pool settings, outlier detection, and TLS mode. They work together: VirtualService references subsets that DestinationRule defines.

hints.title: Think of it as two layers
hints.body: VirtualService = 'route this traffic here'. DestinationRule = 'when traffic reaches that destination, apply these policies'.
